title: Iran announces reopening of Strait of Hormuz
subhead: Iran's foreign minister said the crucial waterway was now fully open to commercial vessels. But US President Donald Trump said the American blockade on Iranian ships and ports 'will remain in full force.'

title: In Lebanon, a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah begins
How Trump learned about the scale of Israel's devastating April 8 strikes on Lebanon
'By firing a publisher, Bolloré united writers, who are usually unpredictable and solitary'
subhead: The dismissal of Olivier Nora from the Grasset publishing house has led to the departure of more than 100 writers. There is no known precedent for such an event, observes Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin.

title: Ukraine and Israel in dispute over Russian ship carrying Ukrainian grain
subhead: Israeli authorities have allowed a Russian vessel suspected of carrying wheat stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock, despite Kyiv's requests to seize the bulk carrier.

title: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
subhead: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

title: Saint-Denis Mayor Bally Bagayoko: 'When you win Paris's largest suburb, it gives you a different standing'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Bally Bagayoko says he can play a role in uniting the left around La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon for the 2027 French presidential election.

Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
subhead: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

title: 'The presence of PFAS in my blood is out of my control': Six Europeans on living in 'forever pollution' hotspots
subhead: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

title: 'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
subhead: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.
